questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A coil of negligible resistance is connected in series with $90 \Omega$ resistor across $120 \mathrm{~V}, 60 \mathrm{~Hz}$ supply. A voltmeter reads $36 \mathrm{~V}$ across resistance. Inductance of the coil is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 36=I_{\mathrm{rms}} \mathrm{R} \\ & 36=\frac{120}{\sqrt{\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2+\mathrm{R}^2}} \times \mathrm{R} \\ & \mathrm{R}=90 \Omega \Rightarrow 36=\frac{120 \times 90}{\sqrt{\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2+90^2}} \\ & \sqrt{\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2+90^2}=300 \\ & \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2=81900 \\ & \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}=286.18 \\ & \omega \mathrm{L}=286.18 \\ & \mathrm{~L}=\frac{286.18}{376.8} \\ & \mathrm{~L}=0.76 \mathrm{H}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: When a $d c$ voltage of $100 \mathrm{~V}$ is applied to an inductor, a $d c$ current of $5 \mathrm{~A}$ flows through it. When an ac voltage of $200 \mathrm{~V}$ peak value is connected to inductor, its inductive reactance is found to be $20 \sqrt{3} \Omega$. The power dissipated in the circuit is _______ W.
questions.solution:
For DC voltage
$\mathrm{R}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{I}}=\frac{100}{5}=20 \Omega$
for AC voltage
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}=20 \sqrt{3} \Omega \\
& \mathrm{R}=20 \Omega \\
& \mathrm{Z}=\sqrt{\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2+\mathrm{R}^2}=\sqrt{3 \times 400+400}=40 \Omega \\
& \text { Power }=\mathrm{i}_{\text {rms }}^2 \mathrm{R} \\
& \quad=\left(\frac{\mathrm{V}_{\text {rms }}}{\mathrm{Z}}\right)^2 \times \mathrm{R}=\left(\frac{\frac{200}{\sqrt{2}}}{40}\right)^2 \times 20=250 \mathrm{~W}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A capacitor of reactance $4 \sqrt{3} \Omega$ and a resistor of resistance $4 \Omega$ are connected in series with an ac source of peak value $8 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~V}$. The power dissipation in the circuit is ________ W.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Z}=\sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{X}^2 \mathrm{~L}} \\ & \mathrm{Z}=\sqrt{4^2+(4 \sqrt{3})^2}=8 \Omega \\ & \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{rms}}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}}=(8 \mathrm{~V}) \\ & \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{rms}}=\frac{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{rms}}}{\mathrm{Z}}=\frac{8}{8}=1 \mathrm{~A}\end{aligned}$ Power dissipated $=\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{rms}}^2 \times \mathrm{R}=1 \times 4=(4 \mathrm{~W})$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In the given figure, the charge stored in 6μF capacitor, when points A and B are joined by a connecting wire is _______μC.
questions.solution:
At steady state, capacitor behaves as an open circuit and current flows in circuit as shown in the diagram.

Req=9 Ω

i=9 V9Ω=1 A

ΔV6Ω=1×6=6 V

VA=3 V

So, potential difference across 6μF is 6 V.

Hence, Q=CΔV

=6×6×10-6 C

=36 μC
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The primary side of a transformer is connected to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>230</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math> supply. The turn ratio of primary to secondary winding is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn></math>. Load resistance connected to the secondary side is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>46</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math>. The power consumed in it is:
questions.solution:
The voltages associated with the primary and the secondary side of the transformer are related to the turn ratio as follows:

V1 V2=N1 N2   ...1

From equation (1), it follows that

230V2=101

V2=23 V

Hence, the power consumed can be calculated as follows

P=V22R=23×2346=11.5 W
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: An alternating emf $\mathrm{E}=110 \sqrt{2} \sin 100 \mathrm{t}$ volt is applied to a capacitor of $2 \mu \mathrm{F}$, the rms value of current in the circuit is _____$\mathrm{mA}$,
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} C & =2 \mu f ; \quad E=110 \sqrt{2} \sin (100 \mathrm{t}) \\ \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{C}} & =\frac{1}{\omega \mathrm{c}}=\frac{1}{100 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}} \\ = & \frac{10000}{2}=5000 \Omega \\ \mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{o}} & =\frac{110 \sqrt{2}}{5000} \\ \mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{rms}} & =\frac{110 \sqrt{2}}{5000 \sqrt{2}} \\ & =\frac{110}{5} \mathrm{~mA} \\ & =22 \mathrm{~mA}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A parallel plate capacitor of capacitance $12.5 \mathrm{pF}$ is charged by a battery connected between its plates to potential difference of $12.0 \mathrm{~V}$. The battery is now disconnected and a dielectric slab $\left(\epsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=6\right)$ is inserted between the plates. The change in its potential energy after inserting the dielectric slab is _____ $10^{-12} \mathrm{~J}$.
questions.solution:
Before inserting dielectric capacitance is given $\mathrm{C}_0=12.5 \mathrm{pF}$ and charge on the capacitor $\mathrm{Q}=\mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}$ After inserting dielectric capacitance will become $\epsilon_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{C}_0$. Change in potential energy of the capacitor $\begin{aligned} & =\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{f}} \\ & =\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{i}}}-\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{f}}}=\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_0}\left[1-\frac{1}{\epsilon_{\mathrm{r}}}\right] \\ & =\frac{\left(\mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}\right)^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_0}\left[1-\frac{1}{\epsilon_{\mathrm{r}}}\right]=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}^2\left[1-\frac{1}{\epsilon_{\mathrm{r}}}\right] \end{aligned}$
Using $\mathrm{C}_0=12.5 \mathrm{pF}, \mathrm{V}=12 \mathrm{~V}, \epsilon_{\mathrm{r}}=6$ $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2}(12.5) \times 12^2\left[1-\frac{1}{6}\right]=\frac{1}{2}(12.5) \times 12^2 \times \frac{5}{6} \\ & =750 \mathrm{pJ}=750 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~J} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Three capacitors of capacitances $25 \mu \mathrm{F}, 30 \mu \mathrm{F}$ and $45 \mu \mathrm{F}$ are connected in parallel to a supply of $100 \mathrm{~V}$. Energy stored in the above combination is E. When these capacitors are connected in series to the same supply, the stored energy is $\frac{9}{x} \mathrm{E}$. The value of $x$ is _____.
questions.solution:
In parallel combination : Potential difference is same across all
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Energy }=\frac{1}{2}\left(\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2+\mathrm{C}_3\right) \mathrm{V}^2 \\
& =\frac{1}{2}(25+30+45) \times(100)^2 \times 10^{-6}=0.5=\mathrm{E}
\end{aligned}$
In series combination: Charge is same on all.
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_{\text {equ }}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_1}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_2}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_3}=\frac{1}{25}+\frac{1}{30}+\frac{1}{45} \\
& \frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_{\text {equ }}}=\frac{(18+15+10)}{450}=\frac{43}{450} \Rightarrow \mathrm{C}_{\text {equ }}=\frac{450}{43} \\
& \text { Energy }=\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_1}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_2}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2 \mathrm{C}_3} \\
& =\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{2}\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_1}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_2}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_3}\right] \\
& \frac{\left(\mathrm{V} \times \mathrm{C}_{\text {equ }}\right)^2}{2} \times \frac{1}{\mathrm{C}_{\text {equ }}}=\frac{\mathrm{V}^2 \mathrm{C}_{\text {equ }}}{2} \\
& \frac{(100)^2}{2} \times \frac{450}{43} \times 10^{-6} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{4.5}{86}=\frac{9}{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{E}=\frac{9}{\mathrm{x}} \times 0.5 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=86
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The electric field between the two parallel plates of a capacitor of $1.5 \mu \mathrm{F}$ capacitance drops to one third of its initial value in $6.6 \mu \mathrm{s}$ when the plates are connected by a thin wire.
The resistance of this wire is _____$\Omega$. (Given, $\log 3=1.1$ )
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{E}=\frac{\mathrm{E}_0}{3} \Rightarrow \mathrm{V}=\frac{\mathrm{V}_0}{3} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{V}_0}{3}=\mathrm{V}_0 \mathrm{e}^{-\frac{\mathrm{t}}{\tau}} \\ & \mathrm{t}=\tau \ell \mathrm{n} 3 \\ & 6.6 \times 10^{-6}=\mathrm{R}\left(1.5 \times 10^{-6}\right)(1.1) \\ & \mathrm{R}=\frac{6}{1.5}=4 \Omega\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> wire is bend to form a square loop. A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> battery with internal resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is connected across one of its sides. If a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>μF</mi></math> capacitor is connected across one of its diagonals, the energy stored by the capacitor will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi>μJ</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math>______.
questions.solution:
Under the balanced state, there is no flow of charge through the capacitor. So, the path connecting the capacitor behaves as an open path.

The equivalent resistance of the entire circuit, under equilibrium condition, can be calculated as follows:

Req=1+114+4+4+14 Ω=1+3 Ω=4 Ω

Hence, the current through the entire circuit is given by

I=VReq=94 A

So, the current I1 can be written as

I1=94×416 A=916 A

The potential difference between the points A and B is,

VA-VB=I1×8=916×8=92 V

Hence, the energy stored in the capacitor is given by

U=12CVA-VB2=12×4×814 μJ=812 μJ

∴x=81
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Twelve wires each having resistance $2 \Omega$ are joined to form a cube. A battery of $6 \mathrm{~V}$ emf is joined across point $a$ and $c$. The voltage difference between $e$ and $f$ is ______V.
questions.solution: From symmetry, current through e-b & g-d =0 $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \mathrm{R}_{\text {eq }}=\frac{3}{4} \times \mathrm{R}=\frac{3}{2} \Omega \\ & \therefore \text { Current through battery }=\frac{6 \times 2}{3}=4 \mathrm{~A} \\ & \mathrm{i}_2=\frac{4}{8} \times 2=1 \mathrm{~A} \\ & \therefore \Delta \mathrm{V} \text { across e-f }=\frac{\mathrm{i}_2}{2} \times \mathrm{R}=\frac{1}{2} \times 2=1 \mathrm{~V} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In the experiment to determine the galvanometer resistance by half-deflection method, the plot of $1 / \theta$ vs the resistance $(\mathrm{R})$ of the resistance box is shown in the figure. The figure of merit of the galvanometer is _____ $\times 10^{-1} \mathrm{~A} /$ division. [The source has emf 2V]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & i=K \theta \\ & \frac{2}{G+R}=K \theta \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\theta}=\frac{(G+R) K}{2}=R\left(\frac{K}{2}\right)+\frac{K G}{2} \\ & \text { Slope }=\frac{K}{2}=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow K=0.5=5 \times 10^{-1} A\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A galvanometer of resistance $100 \Omega$ when connected in series with $400 \Omega$ measures a voltage of upto $10 \mathrm{~V}$. The value of resistance required to convert the galvanometer into ammeter to read upto $10 \mathrm{~A}$ is $x \times 10^{-2} \Omega$. The value of $x$ is :
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{g}}=\frac{10}{400+100}=20 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~A}$
For ammeter
Let shunt resistance $=\mathrm{S}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{g}} \mathrm{R}=\left(\mathrm{i}-\mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{g}}\right) \mathrm{S} \\
& 20 \times 10^{-3} \times 100=10 \mathrm{~S} \\
& \mathrm{~S}=20 \times 10^{-2} \Omega
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A wire of resistance $20 \Omega$ is divided into 10 equal parts, resulting pairs. A combination of two parts are connected in parallel and so on. Now resulting pairs of parallel combination are connected in series. The equivalent resistance of final combination is _____ $\Omega$.
questions.solution: Each part has resistance $=2 \Omega$ 2 parts are connected in parallel so, $\mathrm{R}=1 \Omega$ Now, there will be 5 parts each of resistance $1 \Omega$, they are connected in series. $\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}}=5 \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}}=5 \Omega$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The value of unknown resistance $(x)$ for which the potential difference between $B$ and $D$ will be zero in the arrangement shown, is :
questions.solution: In case of balanced Wheatstone Bridge $\begin{aligned} & \frac{V_{A B}}{V_{A D}}=\frac{V_{B C}}{V_{C D}} \Rightarrow \frac{12}{6+x}=\frac{0.5}{0.5} \\ & x=6 \Omega \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The equivalent resistance between $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ is :
questions.solution: $\Rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}}=6 \Omega+5 \Omega+8 \Omega=19 \Omega$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A galvanmeter has a coil of resistance $200 \Omega$ with a full scale deflection at $20 \mu \mathrm{A}$. The value of resistance to be added to use it as an ammeter of range $(0-20) \mathrm{mA}$ is ;
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{G}=200 \Omega \\ & \mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{g}}=20 \mu \mathrm{A} \\ & \mathrm{i}=\mathrm{i}_{\mathrm{g}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{G}}{\mathrm{S}}+1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow 20 \times 10^{-3}=20 \times 10^{-6}\left(\frac{200}{\mathrm{~S}}+1\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{200}{\mathrm{~S}}=999 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{S} \approx 0.2 \Omega\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The current flowing through the $1 \Omega$ resistor is $\frac{n}{10}$ A. The value of $n$ is _________
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{y-5}{2}+\frac{y-0}{2}+\frac{y-x+10}{1}=0 \\ & y-5+y+2 y-2 x+20=0 \\ & 4 y-2 x+15=0 \quad \ldots .(\text { (i) } \\ & \frac{x-5}{4}+\frac{x-0}{4}+\frac{x-10-y}{1}=0 \\ & x-5+x+4 x-40-4 y=0 \\ & 6 x-4 y-45=0 \quad . .(i) \\ & \frac{-2 x+4 y+15=0 . .(i i)}{4 x-30=0} \\ & x=\frac{15}{2} \& 4 y-15+15=0 \\ & y=0 \\ & i=\frac{y-x+10}{1} \\ & i=\frac{0-7.5+10}{1} \\ & i=2.5 A=\frac{n}{10} A \\ & n=25\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A rod of length $60 \mathrm{~cm}$ rotates with a uniform angular velocity $20 \mathrm{rad} \mathrm{s}^{-1}$ about its perpendicular bisector, in a uniform magnetic filed $0.5 T$. The direction of magnetic field is parallel to the axis of rotation. The potential difference between the two ends of the rod is _____V.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \because \mathrm{V}_0-\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{\mathrm{B} \omega \ell^2}{2} \\ & \mathrm{~V}_0-\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{B}}=\frac{\mathrm{B} \omega \ell^2}{2} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{A}}=\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{B}} \therefore \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{A}}-\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{B}}=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A square loop PQRS having 10 turns, area $3.6 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2$ and resistance $100 \Omega$ is slowly and uniformly being pulled out of a uniform magnetic field of magnitude $\mathrm{B}=0.5 \mathrm{~T}$ as shown. Work done in pulling the loop out of the field in $1.0 \mathrm{~s}$ is ______ $\times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~J}$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \epsilon=N B \ell v \\ & i=\frac{\epsilon}{R}=\frac{N B \ell v}{R} \\ & F=N(i \ell B)=\frac{N^2 B^2 \ell^2 v}{R}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{W}=\mathrm{F} \times \ell=\frac{\mathrm{N}^2 \mathrm{~B}^2 \ell^3}{\mathrm{R}}\left(\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{t}}\right) \\ & \mathrm{A}=\ell^2 \\ & \mathrm{~W}=\frac{(10 \times 10)(0.5)^2 \times\left(3.6 \times 10^{-3}\right)^2}{100 \times 1} \\ & \mathrm{~W}=3.24 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~J}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two coils have mutual inductance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>002</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math>. The current changes in the first coil according to the relation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>i</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mi>s</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>i</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ω</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>50</mn><mi>π</mi></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The maximum value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>emf</mi></math> in the second coil is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mi>α</mi></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
The amount of flux induced in the second coil due to the first one is given by

ϕ2=Mi=Mi0sinωt   ...1

where, M is the mutual inductance of the coils.

The formula to calculate the emf induced can be written as

ε=-dϕ2dt   ...2

From equation (2), it follows that,

εmax=Mi0ω=0.002×5×50π V=π2 V

Hence, α=2.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A square loop of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>7</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is placed vertically in the east-west plane. A uniform magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> is set up across the plane in the north-east direction. The magnetic field is decreased to zero in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> at a steady rate. Then, the magnitude of induced emf is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is _______.
questions.solution:
The area vector for the square loop is given by

A→=(0.1)2 j^ m2

According to the above diagram, the magnetic field is given by

B→=0.22i^+0.22j^

Hence, the magnitude of induced emf can be calculated as follows

e=ΔϕΔt=B→·A→-01=0.22i^+0.22j^·(0.1)2 j^ -01 V=2×10-3 V

Hence, x=2.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A rectangular loop of sides <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>, with its sides parallel to the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math>-axis respectively moves with a velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> in the positive <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> axis direction, in a space containing a variable magnetic field in the positive <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi></math> direction. The field has a gradient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> along the negative <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> direction and it is decreasing with time at the rate of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math> If the resistance of the loop is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi>Ω</mi></math>, the power dissipated by the loop as heat is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Let B0 is the magnetic field at origin. Then,

dBdx=-10-310-2

⇒∫B0BdB=-∫0x10-1dx

⇒B-B0=-10-1x

⇒B=B0-x10

Motional emf in AB=0

Motional emf in CD=0

Motional emf in AD=ε1=B0ℓv

Magnetic field on rod BC 

=B0--12×10-210

Motional emf in  BC=ε2=B0+12×10-210ℓ×v

Net equivalent emf εeq=ε2-ε1=300×10-7 V

For time variation

εeq'=AdBdt=60×10-7 V

εeqnet =εeq+εeq'=360×10-7 V

Power r=εeq net 2R=216×10-9 W
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A small square loop of wire of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math> is placed inside a large square loop of wire of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>L</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>l</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>. The loops are coplanar and their centers coincide. The value of the mutual inductance of the system is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math>______.
questions.solution:
Flux linkage for inner loop

ϕ=Bcenter×l2

=4×μ0i4πL2sin45°+sin45°l2

⇒ϕ=22μ0iπLl2

Therefore, mutual inductance will be

M=ϕi=22μ0l2πL=22μ0π

=224ππ×10-7

=82×10-7

=128×10-7 H

Hence, x=128.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: An infinitely long positively charged straight thread has a linear charge density $\lambda \mathrm{Cm}^{-1}$. An electron revolves along a circular path having axis along the length of the wire. The graph that correctly represents the variation of the kinetic energy of electron as a function of radius of circular path from the wire is :
questions.solution:
Electric field $\mathrm{E}$ at a distance $\mathrm{r}$ due to infinite long wire is $E=\frac{2 k \lambda}{r}$ Force of electron $\Rightarrow F=e E$ $\begin{aligned} & F=e\left(\frac{2 k \lambda}{r}\right) \\ & F=\frac{2 k \lambda e}{r} \end{aligned}$
This force will provide required centripetal force $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{F} & =\frac{\mathrm{m}^2}{\mathrm{r}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{k} \lambda \mathrm{e}}{\mathrm{r}} \\ \mathrm{v} & =\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{k} \lambda \mathrm{e}}{\mathrm{m}}} \\ \mathrm{KE} & =\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m}\left(\frac{2 \mathrm{k} \lambda \mathrm{e}}{\mathrm{m}}\right) \\ & =\mathrm{k} \lambda \mathrm{e} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: An infinite plane sheet of charge having uniform surface charge density $+\sigma_s \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}^2$ is placed on $x-y$ plane. Another infinitely long line charge having uniform linear charge density $+\lambda_e \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}$ is placed at $z=4 \mathrm{~m}$ plane and parallel to $y$-axis. If the magnitude values $\left|\sigma_{\mathrm{s}}\right|=2\left|\lambda_{\mathrm{e}}\right|$ then at point $(0,0,2)$, the ratio of magnitudes of electric field values due to sheet charge to that of line charge is $\pi \sqrt{n}: 1$. The value of $n$ is_______.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{S}}}{\mathrm{E}_{\ell}}=\frac{\sigma}{2 \epsilon_0} \times \frac{2 \pi \epsilon_0 \mathrm{r}}{\lambda} \\ & =\frac{\pi \times \sigma \mathrm{r}}{\lambda} \\ & =\frac{\pi \times 2 \lambda \times 2}{\lambda}=\frac{4 \pi}{1} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{n}=16\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: When a coil is connected across a $20 \mathrm{~V}$ dc supply, it draws a current of $5 \mathrm{~A}$. When it is connected across $20 \mathrm{~V}, 50 \mathrm{~Hz}$ ac supply, it draws a current of $4 \mathrm{~A}$. The self inductance of the coil is ______ $\mathrm{mH}$. $($ Take $\pi=3$ )
questions.solution:
Case-I:
$i=\frac{20}{R} \Rightarrow R=4 \Omega$ Case-II:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{i}=\frac{20}{\mathrm{Z}} \\ & 4=\frac{20}{\sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2}} \Rightarrow \sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2}=5 \\ & \mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}^2=25 \Rightarrow \mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{L}}=3 \Omega \\ & \mathrm{L}=\frac{3}{2 \pi \mathrm{f}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 50}=\frac{1000}{100} \mathrm{mH} \\ & \mathrm{L}=10 \mathrm{mH}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two identical conducting spheres $P$ and $S$ with charge $Q$ on each, repel each other with a force $16 \mathrm{~N}$. A third identical uncharged conducting sphere $R$ is successively brought in contact with the two spheres. The new force of repulsion between $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{S}$ is :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & F_{P S} \propto Q^2 \\ & F_{P S}=16 \mathrm{~N} \end{aligned}$
Now If $\mathrm{P} ~\&~ \mathrm{R}$ are brought in contact then
Now If $S \& R$ are brought in contact then
New force between $P ~\&~ S$ is : $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{PS}} \propto \frac{\mathrm{Q}}{2} \times \frac{3 \mathrm{Q}}{4} \\ & \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{PS}} \propto \frac{3 \mathrm{Q}^2}{8}=\frac{3}{8} \times 16=6 \mathrm{~N} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: An electron is moving under the influence of the electric field of a uniformly charged infinite plane sheet <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> having surface charge density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mi>σ</mi></math>. The electron at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> and has a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The maximum value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>σ</mi></math>, if the electron strikes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mfenced close="]" open="["><mfrac><mrow><mi>m</mi><msub><mi>ϵ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></mrow><mi>e</mi></mfrac></mfenced><mo> </mo><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is _____.
questions.solution:
The value of the electric field due to the uniformly charged plane sheet is given by

E=σ2ε0   ...1

Thus, the force on the electron is given by

F=-eE=-σe2ε0   ...2

So, the acceleration of the electron is given by

a=-σe2ε0 m

Given, u=1 m s-1, t=1 s and S=-1 m

Using the equation of motion, it can be written that

-1=1×1-12×σe2ε0 m×(1)2⇒σ=8ε0 me C m-2

∴α=8.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two identical charged spheres are suspended by strings of equal lengths. The string make an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>37</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with each other. When suspended in a liquid of density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>7</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>, the angle remains same. If density of material of the sphere is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>, the dielectric constant of the liquid is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mn>37</mn><mo>°</mo><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
For first case:

Tcosθ=mg and 

Tsinθ=Fe

Therefore, tanθ=Femg

tanθ=FeρBVg....(i)

For second case:

tanθ=FekρBVg-ρLVg.....(ii) 

From Eq. (i) & (ii), we get

ρBVg=ρB-ρLkVg

⇒1.4 = 0.7 k 

⇒k = 2
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The magnetic field existing in a region is given by $\vec{B}=0.2(1+2 x) \hat{k} \mathrm{~T}$. A square loop of edge $50 \mathrm{~cm}$ carrying 0.5 A current is placed in $x-y$ plane with its edges parallel to the $x-y$ axes, as shown in figure. The magnitude of the net magnetic force experienced by the loop is_____ $\mathrm{mN}$.
questions.solution:
Force on segment parallel to $\mathrm{x}$-axis will cancel each other. Hence $\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{net}}$ will be due to portion parallel to $y$-axis.
$\begin{aligned}
& F=0.5 \times 0.5 \times 6 \times 0.2-0.5 \times 0.5 \times 0.2 \times 5 \\
& =0.5 \times 0.5 \times 0.2 \\
& =0.25 \times 0.2 \\
& =50 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~N} \\
& =50 \mathrm{mN}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two parallel long current carrying wire separated by a distance $2 r$ are shown in the figure. The ratio of magnetic field at $A$ to the magnetic field produced at $C$ is $\frac{x}{7}$. The value of $x$ is _____
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{i}}{2 \pi \mathrm{r}}+\frac{\mu_0(2 \mathrm{i})}{2 \pi(3 \mathrm{r})}=\frac{5 \mu_0 \mathrm{i}}{6 \pi \mathrm{r}} \\ & \mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{\mu_0(2 \mathrm{i})}{2 \pi \mathrm{r}}+\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{i}}{2 \pi(3 \mathrm{r})}=\frac{7 \mu_0 \mathrm{i}}{6 \pi \mathrm{r}} \\ & \therefore \frac{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{C}}}=\frac{5}{7} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{x}=5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A solenoid of length $0.5 \mathrm{~m}$ has a radius of $1 \mathrm{~cm}$ and is made up of ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' number of turns. It carries a current of $5 \mathrm{~A}$. If the magnitude of the magnetic field inside the solenoid is $6.28 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~T}$ then the value of $m$ is _____.
questions.solution:
$\mu_0 n i=B \quad n=$ number of turns per unit length
$\mu_0\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\ell}\right) \mathrm{i}=\mathrm{B}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{m}=\frac{B . \ell}{\mu_0 \mathrm{i}}=\frac{6.28 \times 10^{-3} \times 0.5}{12.56 \times 10^{-7} \times 5} \\ & \mathrm{~m}=500\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
A thin metallic wire having cross sectional area of 10-4 m2 is used to make a ring of radius 30 cm. A positive charge of 2π C is uniformly distributed over the ring, while another positive charge of 30 pC is kept at the centre of the ring. The tension in the ring is _______ N; provided that the ring does not get deformed (neglect the influence of gravity).

(Given, 14πϵ0=9×109 SI units)
questions.solution:
The linear charge density of the ring is given by

λ=Q2πR   ...1

With reference to the above diagram, for the equilibrium condition of the ring, it can be written that

2 Tsindθ2=14πε0q0R2·λRdθ

⇒T=14πε0q0QR2×2π [As for very small angles sindθ2=dθ2]

=9×1092π×30×10-12(0.30)2×2π

=9×10-3×309×10-2=3 N
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A circular coil having 200 turns, $2.5 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2$ area and carrying $100 \mu \mathrm{A}$ current is placed in a uniform magnetic field of 1T. Initially the magnetic dipole moment $(\vec{M})$ was directed along $\vec{B}$. Amount of work, required to rotate the coil through $90^{\circ}$ from its initial orientation such that $\vec{M}$ becomes perpendicular to $\vec{B}$, is ______ $\mu \mathrm{J}$.
questions.solution: We know $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{W}_{\text {ext }} & =\Delta \mathrm{U}+\Delta \mathrm{KE} \quad(\text { P.E. }=-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{M}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}}) \\ & =-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{M}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}}_{\mathrm{f}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{M}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}}_{\mathrm{i}}+0 \\ & =-\mathrm{MB} \cos 90+\mathrm{MB} \cos 0 \\ & =\mathrm{MB} \\ & =\mathrm{NIAB} \\ & =200 \times 100 \times 10^{-6} \times \frac{5}{2} \times 10^{-4} \times 1=5 \mu \mathrm{J} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two long, straight wires carry equal currents in opposite directions as shown in figure. The separation between the wires is 5.0 cm. The magnitude of the magnetic field at a point P midway between the wires is _____ μT. (Given: μ0=4π×10-7 T m A-1)
questions.solution:
The direction of the magnetic field at the observation point due to the current carrying conductor is determined by the right-hand cork screw rule.

From the given diagram, it is clear that as the conductors carry current in the mutually opposite direction, the direction of the magnetic fields produced by them at point P will be the same.

Hence, the required magnetic field at P due to the conductors can be calculated as follows:

B=2×μ0i2πa=2×4π×10-7×102π×52×10-2 T=160×10-6 T=160 μT
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two insulated circular loop A and B radius a carrying a current of I in the anti clockwise direction as shown in figure. The magnitude of the magnetic induction at the centre will be:
questions.solution:
Right hand thumb rule states that, “If you imagine holding a current carrying wire in your right-hand with your thumb pointing towards the direction of electric current flow then the direction in which your fingers curl, gives the direction of lines of force of the magnetic field”. In this case, direciton of magnetic field at the centre for both wires is as shown below.

Magnetic field due to a circular wire is given by, BA=BB=μ0I2a

As both magnetic field lines at centre are perpendicular, therefore

∵Bnet=BA2+BB2=2μ0I2a=μ0I2a
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
A proton moving with a constant velocity passes through a region of space without any change in its velocity. If E→ and B→ represent the electric and magnetic fields respectively, then the region of space may have :
(A) E=0, B=0;  (B) E=0, B≠0;  (C) E≠0, B=0;  (D) E≠0, B≠0

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
Net force on proton inside an electric and a magnetic field is given by

F→e+F→m=qE→+qv→×B→    ...1

If both E→ and B→ are zero, then F→e and F→m both are zero. Hence, velocity may remain constant. Therefore, option (A) is correct.

If E=0, B≠0, but velocity is parallel or antiparallel to the magnetic field, then also F→e and F→m both are zero. Hence, option (B) is also correct.

If E≠0, B≠0, but F→e + F→m=0, then also velocity may remain constant. Thus, option (D) is also correct.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II :

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
(A) Graph between Magnetic susceptibility and magnetising field is :
(B) magnetic field due to a current carrying wire for $\mathrm{x} < \mathrm{a}$ : $\mathrm{B}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{ir}}{2 \pi \mathrm{a}^2}$
(C) magnetic field due to a current carrying wire for $x>a$ : $\mathrm{B}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{i}}{2 \pi \mathrm{a}}$
(D) magnetic field inside solenoid varies as:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A regular polygon of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> sides is formed by bending a wire of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></math> meter. If an electric current of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is flowing through the sides of the polygon, the magnetic field at the centre of the polygon would be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is ______.
questions.solution:
Direction of magnetic field due to all six sides will be in the same direction, therefore we can write 

B=6μ0I4πrsin30°+sin30°, where r=distance of the sides from the centre.

=610-7×4π3π3×3

=72×10-7 T

Hence, x=72.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The coercivity of a magnet is $5 \times 10^3 \mathrm{~A} / \mathrm{m}$. The amount of current required to be passed in a solenoid of length $30 \mathrm{~cm}$ and the number of turns 150 , so that the magnet gets demagnetised when inside the solenoid is _____A.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{c}}=\frac{\mu_{\mathrm{o}} \mathrm{ni}}{\mu_{\mathrm{o}}} \\ & 5 \times 10^3=\frac{150}{30} \times 100 \times \mathrm{i} \\ & \frac{50}{5}=\mathrm{i} \\ & \mathrm{I}=10\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
